name: Old Pali Road
tagline: A paved historic road reclaimed by the jungle — the easiest walk to dramatic Ko'olau cliff views.
parking: Park at the Pali Lookout parking lot ($7 non-resident, free for Hawaii IDs; reservations may be required for non-residents via gostateparks.hawaii.gov)
bestTime: Late afternoon when the light hits the cliffs — but expect wind at the lookout any time of day
kamaaina: Free parking with Hawaii state ID
talkStory: This crumbling roadway was the main route over the Ko'olau Range before the modern Pali Highway tunnels opened in 1961 — and before that, it was the site of the 1795 Battle of Nu'uanu where Kamehameha I's warriors drove hundreds of O'ahu defenders over the 1,000-foot cliffs to their deaths. Human bones have been found at the base of the pali. The old road was itself built over an ancient Hawaiian footpath. Night hikers report it as one of Oahu's most haunted spots, and many locals won't carry pork over the Pali out of respect for the night marchers.
